support.detail: No CEO-irreplaceable act: no blocked discount, no roadmap commitment, no SE/POC guarantee, and no CEO-to-buyer-CEO peer need. The one real gap (Economic Buyer not identified) is fixed by the deal owner's manager engaging Susan to map the approval chain and set up an ROI/exec briefing - a VP/manager move, not the CEO.
support.why_not_vp: Surfacing the Economic Buyer and running an ROI workshop with Susan/Tyler on a $250k early-stage Formal Evaluation is standard second-line sales work; a VP/CRO can do it.

monitor.reason: A doc deliverable we owed Civeo is overdue on a live, high-momentum $250k eval - watch that we do not create our own slip.

triggers.summary: We are 10 days past due on the security + ERP-integration docs Civeo asked us for
triggers.detail: Civeo asked Zycus for detailed documentation on supplier-portal security/offboarding and ERP integration; it was due 23 Jun and is still marked overdue with no evidence it was delivered, and this is our deliverable (not buyer-blocked). On this $250k global S2C deal now in Formal Evaluation with IT (Tyler Scott) and legal actively validating, a stalled security/integration answer is exactly the gap Coupa - already receiving demos - can exploit. If it keeps slipping we hand the buyer a technical objection and slow an otherwise accelerating deal (momentum 70).
triggers.metric: security/ERP-integration docs 10 days overdue (due 23 Jun, still open)
triggers.ceo_ask: Ask Luke whether Civeo now has the supplier-portal security and ERP-integration documentation that was due 23 Jun, and if not, why the only overdue item on a hot $250k eval is on our side.
triggers.evidence: our_open_deliverables: 'Share detailed documentation on supplier portal security/offboarding processes and ERP integration details with Civeo team' due 2026-06-23, status: overdue.
